title: Use the sum identity for sine
text: Recall the sum identity for sine: sin(\alpha+β) $$=$$ sin\alphacos(β) + cos\alphasin(β)

title: Substitute and simplify
text: Substitute the values of sin(\alpha), cos(β), cos(\alpha), and sin(β) into the sum identity and simplify.

title: Calculate sin(\alpha+β)
text: Using sin(\alpha) $$=$$ $$\frac{3}{5}$$, cos(\alpha) $$=$$ $$\frac{4}{5}$$, sin(β) $$=$$ $$\frac{-12}{13}$$, cos(β) $$=$$ $$\frac{-5}{13}$$, what is sin(\alpha+β)?
